description: §41.15.3 (OQ-SCH-3) — multiple schemaFor(T) calls compose inside a single <schema> block via concatenation (schemaFor(User) + schemaFor(Post) in one block produce two table-declaration fragments). Each call emits a BARE table-declaration fragment (NOT a wrapping <schema>...</schema>), so N calls concatenate into one schema. STRUCTURAL-EFFECT-VIA-CODES: the two emitted table-declaration fragments are impl-freedom + compile-time-only (§41.15.9), so composition is asserted by ACCEPTANCE — two struct-derived tables in one block expand without any E-SCHEMAFOR-* fire. Were the bare-fragment emit shape (§41.15.2) broken (e.g. each call emitting a full nested <schema>), a second call would collide, so a clean multi-call compile is the impl-neutral proof the fragment-concatenation model holds.
rationale: §41.15.2 + §41.15.3 — schemaFor(T) SHALL produce a bare <schema> table-declaration fragment (table name + columns + constraints), NOT a wrapping <schema> block; multiple schemaFor(T) calls SHALL be composable inside a single <schema> block via concatenation. The impl-neutral contract for the composition path is the acceptance of N calls in one block with no E-SCHEMAFOR-* diagnostic; the emitted concatenated DDL text is impl-freedom (§41.15.2 round-trip property) and is a compile-time artifact with no runtime DOM/state projection (§41.15.9).
